forecast-period.text: A series of low pressure systems passing over waters to the south will extend cold fronts that will cross over SA through to the end of the week. A high pressure system is then forecast to move into the Bight from late Friday and extend a ridge across the state over the weekend, as it slowly moves southeastwards towards Tasmania.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones. Marine forecasts are also available on VHF and HF radio. View the radio schedule on www.bom.gov.au/marine
Check the latest Coastal Waters Forecasts for information on wind, wave and weather conditions for neighbouring coastal zones.

area.description: Far West Coast: WA-SA Border to Fowlers Bay

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Thursday for Far West Coast
forecast-period.text: Westerly 20 to 25 knots, reaching up to 30 knots during the morning and early afternoon. Winds turning southwesterly 15 to 25 knots during the morning and early afternoon.
2 to 3 metres.
Southwesterly 2.5 to 3 metres, increasing to 3 to 5 metres during the morning.
Cloudy. 90% chance of showers.
Southwesterly 15 to 20 knots turning south to southeasterly below 10 knots during the morning.
1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing below 1 metre during the morning.
Southwesterly 4 to 5 metres.
Cloudy.
Easterly 10 to 15 knots.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 3 to 4 metres, decreasing to 2 to 3 metres during the morning.
Southerly 1 to 2 metres, decreasing below 0.5 metres during the evening.
Cloudy. 50% chance of showers.

area.description: Upper West Coast: Fowlers Bay to Elliston

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Thursday for Upper West Coast
forecast-period.text: Westerly 25 to 30 knots turning southwesterly 20 to 30 knots in the late afternoon.
2 to 3 metres.
Westerly 2 to 4 metres, tending southwesterly 4 to 6 metres during the morning.
Partly cloudy. 95% chance of showers.
Southwesterly 15 to 20 knots becoming variable about 10 knots in the middle of the day.
1 to 2 metres, decreasing to 1 metre during the morning.
Southwesterly 4 to 6 metres, decreasing to 4 metres later in the evening.
Cloudy.
Southeasterly below 10 knots tending easterly 10 to 15 knots during the morning.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 3 to 4 metres, decreasing to 2.5 to 3 metres during the morning.
Southerly 1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing below 0.5 metres during the evening.
Cloudy.

area.description: Lower West Coast: Elliston to Cape Carnot

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Thursday for Lower West Coast
forecast-period.text: Westerly 25 to 30 knots turning southwesterly 20 to 30 knots in the evening.
2.5 to 3 metres.
Westerly 3 to 5 metres, tending southwesterly 5 to 6 metres during the afternoon.
Partly cloudy. 90% chance of showers.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Friday for Lower West Coast
forecast-period.text: Southwesterly 15 to 25 knots, reaching up to 30 knots early in the morning. Winds decreasing to 10 to 15 knots in the morning then becoming southerly below 10 knots in the late afternoon.
2 to 2.5 metres, decreasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the morning, then decreasing below 1 metre around midday.
Southwesterly 4 to 6 metres, decreasing to 4 metres later in the evening.
Cloudy.
Variable below 10 knots becoming easterly 10 to 15 knots during the afternoon.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 3 to 4 metres, decreasing to 3 metres during the morning.
Cloudy. 50% chance of showers.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Gale Warning for Thursday for Central Coast
forecast-period.text: Westerly 25 to 30 knots, reaching up to 35 knots in the afternoon. Winds turning southwesterly in the evening.
3 metres.
Westerly 3 to 5 metres, increasing to 4 to 7 metres around midday, then tending southwesterly 6 to 7 metres by early evening.
Cloudy. 95% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm this morning.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Friday for Central Coast
forecast-period.text: Southwesterly 15 to 25 knots, reaching up to 30 knots early in the morning. Winds decreasing to about 10 knots in the late afternoon.
2 to 3 metres, decreasing to 1 to 2 metres during the morning, then decreasing below 1 metre by early evening.
Southwesterly 5 to 7 metres, decreasing to 4 to 5 metres by early evening.
Cloudy. 60% chance of showers.
Southerly about 10 knots tending southeasterly 10 to 15 knots during the morning then tending easterly during the evening.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 3 to 4 metres, decreasing to 3 metres during the afternoon.
Cloudy. 50% chance of showers.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Gale Warning for Thursday for South Central Coast
forecast-period.text: Westerly 30 to 35 knots.
3 metres.
Westerly 3 to 5 metres, increasing to 4 to 7 metres around midday, then tending southwesterly 6 to 7 metres by early evening.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm during the morning and afternoon.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Gale Warning for Friday for South Central Coast
forecast-period.text: Southwesterly 20 to 30 knots, reaching up to 35 knots early in the morning. Winds decreasing to 15 to 25 knots in the morning.
2.5 to 4 metres, decreasing to 1.5 to 2.5 metres during the morning, then decreasing to 1 metre by early evening.
Southwesterly 5 to 7 metres, decreasing to 5 metres by early evening.
Cloudy. 80% chance of showers.
South to southwesterly 10 to 15 knots turning southeasterly during the day.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 3 to 4 metres.
Cloudy.

area.description: Upper South East Coast: Murray Mouth to Cape Jaffa

forecast-period.warning-summary: Gale Warning for Thursday for Upper South East Coast
forecast-period.text: Northwesterly 30 to 35 knots turning westerly during the morning and early afternoon.
2.5 to 3 metres.
Westerly 2 to 3 metres, increasing to 3 to 5 metres around midday, then tending west to southwesterly 5 to 6 metres later in the evening.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm south of Salt Creek. Possible small hail late this afternoon and evening.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Gale Warning for Friday for Upper South East Coast
forecast-period.text: West to southwesterly 20 to 30 knots, reaching up to 35 knots early in the morning. Winds becoming southwesterly 15 to 25 knots in the middle of the day.
3 to 4 metres, decreasing to 1.5 to 2.5 metres during the morning, then decreasing to 1 metre by early evening.
Southwesterly 5 to 7 metres, decreasing to 5 metres later in the evening.
Cloudy. 90% chance of showers.
South to southwesterly 10 to 15 knots turning southeasterly during the afternoon.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 3 to 5 metres.
Cloudy.

area.description: Lower South East Coast: Cape Jaffa to SA-VIC Border

forecast-period.warning-summary: Gale Warning for Thursday for Lower South East Coast
forecast-period.text: Northwesterly 30 to 35 knots turning westerly 30 to 40 knots during the morning and early afternoon.
3 metres, increasing to 3 to 5 metres south of Beachport.
Westerly 2 to 4 metres, increasing to 4 to 6 metres around midday, then increasing to 6 to 8 metres by early evening.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm. Possible small hail late this afternoon and evening.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Gale Warning for Friday for Lower South East Coast
forecast-period.text: Westerly 25 to 30 knots, reaching up to 35 knots early in the morning. Winds tending southwesterly before dawn then decreasing to 15 to 25 knots in the middle of the day.
2.5 to 4 metres, decreasing to 1 to 2 metres during the afternoon.
Westerly 7 to 8 metres, tending southwesterly 6 to 8 metres during the morning, then decreasing to 5 to 6 metres during the afternoon.
Cloudy. 95% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm south of Port MacDonnell in the early morning. Possible small hail south of Beachport in the early morning.
South to southwesterly 15 to 20 knots tending south to southeasterly 10 to 15 knots during the evening.
1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing to 1 metre during the afternoon.
Southwesterly 4 to 5 metres.
Cloudy. 50% chance of showers.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Thursday for Spencer Gulf
forecast-period.text: Northwesterly 20 to 30 knots turning westerly during the morning.
1 to 2 metres, increasing to 2 to 3 metres south of Cowell to Wallaroo.
South to southwesterly below 1 metre, increasing to 2 to 3 metres south of Cowell to Wallaroo during the afternoon.
Cloudy. 95% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm south of Port Neill to Port Victoria early this morning.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Friday for Spencer Gulf
forecast-period.text: West to southwesterly 15 to 20 knots, reaching up to 30 knots south of Cowell to Wallaroo early in the morning. Winds tending south to southwesterly below 10 knots during the day.
2 to 3 metres, decreasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the morning, then decreasing below 1 metre by early evening.
South to southwesterly 2 to 3 metres, increasing to 2 to 4 metres south of Port Neill to Port Victoria.
Cloudy.
Variable about 10 knots becoming southeasterly 10 to 15 knots during the afternoon.
Below 1 metre.
South to southwesterly 2 to 3 metres, decreasing to 1 to 2 metres during the afternoon.
Cloudy.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Gale Warning for Thursday for Gulf St Vincent
forecast-period.text: Northwesterly 20 to 30 knots, reaching up to 35 knots south of Port Vincent to Brighton early in the morning. Winds turning westerly in the middle of the day.
2 to 3 metres.
South to southwesterly below 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres south of Port Vincent to Brighton later in the evening.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of rain.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Friday for Gulf St Vincent
forecast-period.text: Westerly 20 to 25 knots, reaching up to 30 knots south of Port Vincent to Brighton early in the morning. Winds turning southwesterly 15 to 25 knots early in the morning then decreasing to 10 to 15 knots in the middle of the day.
2 to 3 metres, decreasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the morning, then decreasing below 1 metre during the afternoon.
South to southwesterly 1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing to around 1 metre during the morning.
Cloudy. 50% chance of showers.
Variable below 10 knots becoming southeasterly 10 to 15 knots during the afternoon.
Below 1 metre.
West to southwesterly below 0.5 metres.
Partly cloudy.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Gale Warning for Thursday for Investigator Strait
forecast-period.text: Westerly 25 to 30 knots, reaching up to 35 knots in the afternoon.
2.5 to 3 metres.
Westerly 2 to 3 metres, increasing to 2 to 4 metres by early evening, then tending west to southwesterly 2 to 4 metres later in the evening.
Cloudy. 95% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm this morning.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Friday for Investigator Strait
forecast-period.text: Westerly 20 to 25 knots, reaching up to 30 knots early in the morning. Winds turning southwesterly 15 to 25 knots before dawn then decreasing to 10 to 15 knots in the middle of the day.
2 to 3 metres, decreasing to 1 to 2 metres during the morning, then decreasing below 1 metre by early evening.
West to southwesterly 2 to 4 metres, decreasing to 2 to 3 metres during the morning, then decreasing to 1 to 2 metres by early evening.
Cloudy. 50% chance of showers.
South to southwesterly about 10 knots tending southeasterly 10 to 15 knots during the day.
Below 1 metre.
West to southwesterly 1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing to around 1 metre during the afternoon.
Cloudy.
